---
title: "Market Concentration and Export Resilience"
author:
  - name: "Ana Ribeiro"
    email: "ana.ribeiro@example.org"
    corresponding: true
    affiliation:
      - "1"
  - name: "Jonas Meyer"
    affiliation:
      - "1"
      - "2"
affiliations:
  - id: "1"
    name: "Institute for Quantitative Policy Research"
  - id: "2"
    name: "University of Cologne"
abstract: |
  This article-style starter examines whether more concentrated export markets
  recover faster after external shocks. It is intentionally compact while still
  showing the metadata, section structure, and academic framing expected in a
  journal-oriented manuscript.
bibliography: references.bib
format:
  typstR-typst: default
typstR:
  format-variant: article
  keywords:
    - export resilience
    - market concentration
    - industrial organization
  jel:
    - L11
    - F14
  acknowledgements: |
    The authors thank colleagues for feedback on the research design and
    interpretation of the baseline results.
  funding: |
    This research was supported by the Competitive Markets Initiative.
---

## Introduction

Use the introduction to define the research question, motivate the article for
an academic audience, and situate the contribution in the related literature.
@smith2020

## Data and Methods

Briefly describe the dataset, the identification strategy, and any key model or
estimation choices required to reproduce the main analysis.

## Results

Present the primary findings with enough narrative to show how tables and
figures would fit into the article workflow.

## Conclusion

End with the paper's contribution, external validity, and the most important
avenue for follow-up work.

## References
